mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future earnings and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes over a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points within a single month signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and triggers negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 55% of instances where UNRATE spikes above the threshold, or the signal loses predictive power during expansionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand recovery, which boosts corporate profit margins and drives sustained positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.04, or housing market decoupling breaks the correlation.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production signals supply-side bottlenecks and reduced manufacturing output, which compresses corporate earnings and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the following week during inflationary periods.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are not significantly negative when INDPRO declines during CPIAUCSL growth above 3%, or the mechanism shows positive returns instead.

mechanism_narrative: A falling federal funds rate combined with stabilizing consumer price inflation indicates successful monetary easing without reigniting price pressures, which improves real discount rates and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative 20-day returns in over 60% of observations, or CPIAUCSL acceleration negates the rate cut effect.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising household income, which supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward returns over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in real gross domestic product exceeding 0.5% confirms economic contraction, which immediately triggers risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of quarters where GDPC1 falls below the threshold, or the signal fails to predict direction during recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside stable retail sales indicates efficient supply chain utilization without demand destruction, which improves corporate operating leverage and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next month during normal growth cycles.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 20-day returns in over 55% of cases, or the mechanism breaks down when supply shocks dominate demand trends.
